content:
Council
6. (1) The affairs of the Corporation shall be administered by a
Council to be constituted in accordance with the rules and by-laws
of the Corporation.
(2) The first members of the Council shall be those persons who
immediately before the appointed day are the members of the
Council constituted under subsection 6(1) of the Federation of
Malaya Red Cross Society (Incorporation) Act 1962.
Common seal of the Corporation

Common seal of the Corporation
7. (1) The Corporation shall have a common seal, and such seal
may from time to time be broken, changed, altered and made anew
as the Corporation deems fit.
(2) All deeds, documents and other instruments requiring the
seal of the Corporation shall be sealed with the seal of the Corporation
pursuant to a resolution of the Council to that effect, in the presence
of the Chairman or a Vice-Chairman of the Council, and of the
Secretary of the same, or some other person authorized by the
Council to act in that behalf, who shall sign every such deed,
document, or other instrument to which such seal is affixed, and
such signing shall be sufficient evidence that such seal was duly
and properly affixed and that the same was the lawful seal of the
Corporation.
8
Laws of Malaysia
ACT 540
Register of members

Register of members
8. (1) The Council shall cause a register to be kept in which the
name of every person who is, immediately before the appointed
day, a member of the former Corporation or of any Branch, or who
may thereafter become a duly admitted member of the Corporation
and in which shall be recorded such other matters as may from
time to time be determined by the Council.
(2) The register may be kept in such number of parts as the
Council may from time to time decide and the duty of keeping
parts of the register may be delegated to any member or official
of the Corporation.
Rules and by-laws
9.
It shall be lawful for the Council from time to time to make,
alter or revoke such rules and by-laws as it may deem expedient
for the management of the affairs of the Corporation and the
accomplishment of its objects, and all such rules and by-laws shall
be binding upon the members of the Corporation.
10.
(Omitted).
Transfer of property and liabilities, etc.

Transfer of property and liabilities, etc.
11. (1) On the appointed day all property belonging to the former
Corporation or any Branch, whether held in the name of the former
Corporation or that Branch, or in the name or names of any person
or persons in trust for the former Corporation or that Branch, shall
be and the same is hereby vested in the Corporation, and the same
together with all after-acquired property, movable or immovable,
and all subscriptions, contributions, donations, fines, amounts of
loans and advances received or to be received shall be held by the
Corporation for the purposes of this Act and subject to the rules
of the Corporation.
(2) All debts and liabilities of the former Corporation or any
Branch existing immediately before the appointed day shall be
paid by the Corporation, and all debts due to and subscriptions and
contributions payable to the former Corporation or any Branch
shall be paid to the Corporation for the purposes of this Act.
9
Malaysian Red Crescent Society (Incorporation)
Application of income and property

Application of income and property
12. (1) The income and property of the Corporation, whencesoever
derived, shall be applied solely towards the promotion of the
objects of the Corporation as set out in this Act, and no portion
thereof shall be paid or transferred directly or indirectly by way
of dividend, bonus or otherwise howsoever by way of profit to the
members of the Corporation:
Provided that nothing herein contained shall prevent the payment
in good faith of remuneration to any officers or servants of the
Corporation or to any members of the Corporation in return for
any services actually rendered to the Corporation.
(2) The liability of each member of the Corporation in respect
of the transactions of the Corporation shall in no case exceed the
amount of such annual subscriptions as may be due from such
member to the Corporation.
Saving
13.
Nothing in this Act shall affect the rights of the Yang
di-Pertuan Agong, the Rulers or the Yang di-Pertua Negeri or of
any body politic or corporate or of any other persons except such
as are mentioned in this Act and those claiming by, from or under
them.
Repeal
14.
The Federation of Malaya Red Cross Society (Incorporation)
Act 1962 is hereby repealed.
10
